task.title: Phase 4 Exercise: Building a Custom DITA-OT Plugin
task.shortdesc: Create a complete HTML5 customization plugin with XSLT overrides, CSS branding, and DITAVAL filtering — from scaffold to production. Primary track for software engineers, optional for advanced writers.

ul.li: Completed Phase 3E exercise (understand the DITA-OT pipeline)
Read Part 8 topics: Create HTML5 Plugin, XSLT Customization Tutorial, Safe Customization Strategies
Basic familiarity with XSLT (read the XSLT Prerequisites topic if needed)

context.p: You will build a real plugin called com.taskflow.html5 that customizes HTML5 output with branded styling, note callouts, and step enhancements. This follows the same pattern used in production DITA deployments.

info.codeblock: mkdir -p com.taskflow.html5/xsl/html5 mkdir -p com.taskflow.html5/css mkdir -p com.taskflow.html5/js
com.taskflow.html5/ ├── plugin.xml ← Plugin descriptor ├── xsl/ │ └── html5/ │ └── override.xsl ← XSLT overrides ├── css/ │ └── brand.css ← Custom styles └── js/ └── custom.js ← Optional JavaScript

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <plugin id="com.taskflow.html5"> <!-- Require the base HTML5 plugin --> <require plugin="org.dita.html5"/>
<!-- Override XSLT --> <feature extension="dita.xsl.html5" file="xsl/html5/override.xsl"/>
<!-- Add custom CSS --> <feature extension="args.css" file="css/brand.css"/> <feature extension="args.cssroot" file="css"/>
<transtype name="taskflow-html5" extends="html5"> <param name="nav-toc" type="enum"> <val default="true">full</val> </param> </transtype> </plugin>
info.note: The <require> element ensures your plugin loads after the HTML5 base plugin. The <feature> elements hook into extension points — they do NOT replace the originals, they extend them via XSLT import priority.

info.codeblock: callout callout- note
info.p: What this does: Replaces the default note rendering with a <div class="callout callout-tip"> (or warning, caution, etc.) so CSS can style each type differently.

step.cmd: Install your plugin and build with it.

info.codeblock:
# Copy plugin to DITA-OT plugins directory cp -r com.taskflow.html5 "$(dita --install-dir)/plugins/"
# Integrate (registers the plugin) dita --install
# Build using your custom transtype dita -i taskflow-docs/taskflow.ditamap \ -f taskflow-html5 \ -o out/taskflow-branded

ul.li: Notes rendered as colored callout boxes (blue, green, orange, red)

step.cmd: Extend your XSLT to add anchor links to task steps.

info.p: Add this template to override.xsl (before the closing </xsl:stylesheet>):
Rebuild and verify that steps with id attributes get anchor-friendly HTML.

step.cmd: Test your plugin against the existing lab sample project.

info.codeblock: # Build the exercises lab-sample with your plugin dita -i ../exercises/lab-sample.ditamap \ -f taskflow-html5 \ -o out/lab-test
info.p: The lab sample has all four note types. This validates your plugin works with content you did not author.

stepresult.p: All notes should render as styled callouts. If any fail, check your XSLT class matching and rebuild.

step.cmd: Practice debugging: intentionally break the XSLT and fix it.

ol.li: Remove the closing </xsl:attribute> tag from the note template
Fix the error, rebuild, and verify

info.note: XSLT errors from DITA-OT can be cryptic. Key debugging strategies: (1) always check the temp directory for preprocessed files, (2) use --debug for verbose logging, (3) simplify your override to isolate the problem.

p.b: Self-Check — you should now be able to:

ul.li: ✓ Create a DITA-OT plugin from scratch with proper structure
✓ Write plugin.xml with extension points and dependencies
✓ Override XSLT templates using safe import patterns
✓ Add custom CSS to branded output
✓ Install, integrate, and test a plugin
✓ Debug XSLT errors in DITA-OT builds

result.note: Next step: explore the com.extense.html5.labs starter plugin in the exercises/plugins/ directory for more advanced patterns including TOC overrides and sidebar toggles.
